vision_report.inventory_reconciliation: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding current biological viability. P2 and P4 are no longer visible as living specimens; the soil appears barren or contains only debris.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot shows no visible foliage; soil appears dark and undisturbed.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot (shared) shows no signs of Mexican Mint; appears to be a systemic loss.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen remains, though it shows significant postural decline and leaf loss over the sequence.
plant_audit.P4: Silver Guest is not identifiable; presumed lost.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark and moist-appearing throughout the sequence.
biome_observations.anomalies: Presence of crushed eggshells in P3/P4 area; white rabbit anchor remains stable; white paper/cable debris introduced in later frames.
biome_observations.fungal_presence: None detected, though soil moisture levels appear high.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Initial state shows a single green leaf in the P3/P4 pot.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Gradual chlorosis and postural collapse of the remaining P3 leaf.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_current: The P3 leaf has withered significantly, losing its structural integrity and turgor pressure.

visual_health_inference.P3: Critical/Terminal. The specimen is suffering from severe dehydration or root rot, evidenced by the loss of leaf turgor and eventual collapse.
visual_health_inference.overall_biome: The environment is failing to support the registered specimens.

anomalies: Introduction of white cable/wire in T-1 and Current frames.
Persistent presence of eggshell fragments as a soil amendment/top dressing.
Total loss of visible foliage in P1, P2, and P4.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is in a state of advanced decline. The Pothos (P3) has transitioned from a healthy, upright state to a collapsed, necrotic state over the 5-day observation period. The other registered plants (P1, P2, P4) are not visible, suggesting either total mortality or complete burial. The environment is cluttered with non-botanical debris (cables, paper), and the lack of visible growth suggests a failure in the current maintenance protocol.
